title: hieracium lanifolium subsp. colmeiroanum
intro: Hieracium lanifolium subsp. colmeiroanum, also known as Colmeiro's hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium lanifolium plant, which belongs to the dandelion family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. Colmeiro's hawkweed has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and acting as a natural diuretic, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Colmeiro's hawkweed can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions to respiratory issues.
